text: Emergence of Smart Traffic Management System Using Deep Learning and IoT Smart traffic management system is a must for today’s ever growing cities to curb traffic congestion and to minimize loss of life and fuel. Most of the cities around the globe utilize traffic management systems based on programming logic control (PLC) and round robin algorithms to manage traffic flow. With the advent of Internet of Things (IoT) and deep learning the present traffic management systems can be improved to a great extent. Recent works on smart traffic management system relies heavily on IoT and uses traffic density of each lane to manage the flow of traffic. However, this system is not accurate as the models used in the system require large amount of emergency vehicle image datasets which is not available in the present times. In this paper, we propose a distributed IoT framework for real time traffic management based on two fundamental assumptions. The first assumption is that the infrastructure of the roads and lanes do not change frequently. Using this assumption, we propose a two stage vehicle detector that achieves about 77% detection accuracy on UA-DETRAC dataset. The second assumption is that emergency vehicles can be identified by a unique audio signal of their sirens. We propose a novel acoustic detection technique using an edge device that achieves about 99.4% accuracy in detecting emergency vehicles.
explanation: Well-structured and uses domain-specific details (UA-DETRAC, IoT). The unusually exact accuracy claims (e.g., 99.4%) and overly polished tone hint at possible AI generation.
